title: Installing and Managing Packages
description: Install, update, and remove dependencies with npm.

slides.title: Installing Packages 📥
slides.content:
# Installing Packages

## Basic install
```bash
npm install <package-name>
```
This installs the package and adds it to `dependencies` in `package.json`. A `node_modules` folder is created (if not exists) and the package is placed there.

Example:
```bash
npm install lodash
```

Now you can use it in your code:
```javascript
const _ = require('lodash');
console.log(_.camelCase('hello world')); // helloWorld
```

slides.title: Dev Dependencies 🛠️
slides.content:
## Installing for Development Only

Some packages are only needed during development (testing, building). Install them with `--save-dev` (or `-D`):

```bash
npm install jest --save-dev
```

This adds to `devDependencies` in `package.json`.

**Why separate?** When you deploy your app to production, you can run `npm install --production` to install only `dependencies`, saving space and time.

slides.title: Install Shortcuts
slides.codeSnippet:
npm install express          # adds to dependencies
npm install -D nodemon       # adds to devDependencies (shorthand)
npm install -g nodemon       # install globally (not in package.json)
npm install                  # install all dependencies from package.json

slides.title: Updating and Removing Packages 🔄
slides.content:
## Updating Packages

Check for outdated packages:
```bash
npm outdated
```

Update all packages according to version rules:
```bash
npm update
```

Update a specific package:
```bash
npm install lodash@latest
```

## Removing Packages

```bash
npm uninstall lodash
```
This removes the package from `node_modules` and from `dependencies` (or `devDependencies`).

quizData.question: What flag would you use to install a package as a devDependency?
quizData.explanation: `--save-dev` and its shorthand `-D` both install as devDependencies.

slides.title: ⚠️ node_modules size
slides.content:
## node_modules can be huge

It's common for `node_modules` to contain hundreds of megabytes. Never commit it to Git. Always add `node_modules/` to your `.gitignore` file.

Example `.gitignore`:
```
node_modules/
.env
```
